perturbation.original_text: 5. This Agreement applies to all Confidential Information that is disclosed by the Company to Recipient during the period that begins on the Effective Date. The obligations of this Agreement will remain in effect for as long as the Confidential Information remains confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of the Company. Or, if the Parties enter into a subsequent agreement governing the disclosure of Confidential Information, this Agreement will terminate and that subsequent agreement shall control the use and disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. This Agreement applies to all Confidential Information that is disclosed by the Company to Recipient during the period that begins on the Effective Date. The obligations of this Agreement will remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date, regardless of whether the Confidential Information remains confidential, proprietary, or a trade secret. Or, if the Parties enter into a subsequent agreement governing the disclosure of Confidential Information, this Agreement will terminate and that subsequent agreement shall control the use and disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the duration of the NDA to two years, contradicting the original clause which stated the agreement remains in effect as long as the information remains confidential. This creates ambiguity about the agreement's lifespan.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement applies to all Confidential Information that is disclosed by the Company to Recipient during the period that begins on the Effective Date. The obligations of this Agreement will remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date, regardless of whether the Confidential Information remains confidential, proprietary, or a trade secret. Or, if the Parties enter into a subsequent agreement governing the disclosure of Confidential Information, this Agreement will terminate and that subsequent agreement shall control the use and disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5 to limit the NDA's duration to two years. This directly contradicts the original agreement, which stated the NDA remains in effect as long as the information remains confidential. The Contradicted Text is a copy of the Changed text, as a result of the change there is an in-text contradiction in the document, and therefore it is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 1. “Confidential Information ” shall mean information including, but not limited to, the existence of the discussions between the Company and Recipient or any third-party regarding future business relationships and information regarding the Company’s products, services, product designs, plans, roadmaps, prices, costs, trade secrets, inventions, intellectual property, development plans, tooling, equipment, import or export controls, license arrangements or agreements, methods, techniques, proprietary processes and know-how, programs, schematics, software, data, customer lists, financial information, inside information (including information regarding financial performance, earnings, existing products, existing techniques, new products, new techniques and business strategies), personnel information (including, without limitation, skills and compensation), product development information, client development information, information regarding possible acquisitions or sales of businesses or facilities sales, marketing plans, business opportunities, research and development activities, pre-release products, information posted on the Company’s web site (to the extent that such information is not publicly accessible), or any other information which the Recipient knows or reasonably should know is confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of the Company. This definition also includes any Confidential Information disclosed by or to any affiliate or subsidiary of the Company. Recipient understands and agrees that Company’s client and prospective client lists, proposals, reports, all internal memoranda and documents and any information communicated to Recipient, written or oral, that is indicated as confidential, are trade secrets within the meaning of Chapter 19.108 of the Revised Code of Washington, and that misappropriation of such information, as defined in said Chapter, shall subject Recipient to penalties as set forth in said Chapter. Confidential Information shall not include information (i) which was lawfully in the possession of Recipient prior to disclosure of such information by the Company; (ii) which was, or at any time becomes, available in the public domain other than through a violation of this Agreement; (iii) which is documented, to the satisfaction of both Parties, by Recipient as having -2- been developed by Recipient independently; or (iv) which is furnished to Recipient or its representatives by a third party not under an obligation of and without a violation of confidentiality to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. “Confidential Information ” shall mean information including, but not limited to, the existence of the discussions between the Company and Recipient or any third-party regarding future business relationships and information regarding the Company’s products, services, product designs, plans, roadmaps, prices, costs, trade secrets, inventions, intellectual property, development plans, tooling, equipment, import or export controls, license arrangements or agreements, methods, techniques, proprietary processes and know-how, programs, schematics, software, data, customer lists, financial information, inside information (including information regarding financial performance, earnings, existing products, existing techniques, new products, new techniques and business strategies), personnel information (including, without limitation, skills and compensation), product development information, client development information, information regarding possible acquisitions or sales of businesses or facilities sales, marketing plans, business opportunities, research and development activities, pre-release products, information posted on the Company’s web site (to the extent that such information is not publicly accessible), or any other information which the Recipient actually knows is confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of the Company. This definition also includes any Confidential Information disclosed by or to any affiliate or subsidiary of the Company. Recipient understands and agrees that Company’s client and prospective client lists, proposals, reports, all internal memoranda and documents and any information communicated to Recipient, written or oral, that is indicated as confidential, are trade secrets within the meaning of Chapter 19.108 of the Revised Code of Washington, and that misappropriation of such information, as defined in said Chapter, shall subject Recipient to penalties as set forth in said Chapter. Confidential Information shall not include information (i) which was lawfully in the possession of Recipient prior to disclosure of such information by the Company; (ii) which was, or at any time becomes, available in the public domain other than through a violation of this Agreement; (iii) which is documented, to the satisfaction of both Parties, by Recipient as having -2- been developed by Recipient independently; or (iv) which is furnished to Recipient or its representatives by a third party not under an obligation of and without a violation of confidentiality to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'or reasonably should know' is omitted, creating a contradiction. Now, information the recipient 'reasonably should know' is confidential is no longer protected, despite the general intent. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 1, because the original definition intended a broader scope of confidential information by including what a reasonable person would recognize as confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information ” shall mean information including, but not limited to, the existence of the discussions between the Company and Recipient or any third-party regarding future business relationships and information regarding the Company’s products, services, product designs, plans, roadmaps, prices, costs, trade secrets, inventions, intellectual property, development plans, tooling, equipment, import or export controls, license arrangements or agreements, methods, techniques, proprietary processes and know-how, programs, schematics, software, data, customer lists, financial information, inside information (including information regarding financial performance, earnings, existing products, existing techniques, new products, new techniques and business strategies), personnel information (including, without limitation, skills and compensation), product development information, client development information, information regarding possible acquisitions or sales of businesses or facilities sales, marketing plans, business opportunities, research and development activities, pre-release products, information posted on the Company’s web site (to the extent that such information is not publicly accessible), or any other information which the Recipient actually knows is confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of the Company. This definition also includes any Confidential Information disclosed by or to any affiliate or subsidiary of the Company. Recipient understands and agrees that Company’s client and prospective client lists, proposals, reports, all internal memoranda and documents and any information communicated to Recipient, written or oral, that is indicated as confidential, are trade secrets within the meaning of Chapter 19.108 of the Revised Code of Washington, and that misappropriation of such information, as defined in said Chapter, shall subject Recipient to penalties as set forth in said Chapter. Confidential Information shall not include information (i) which was lawfully in the possession of Recipient prior to disclosure of such information by the Company; (ii) which was, or at any time becomes, available in the public domain other than through a violation of this Agreement; (iii) which is documented, to the satisfaction of both Parties, by Recipient as having -2- been developed by Recipient independently; or (iv) which is furnished to Recipient or its representatives by a third party not under an obligation of and without a violation of confidentiality to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'or reasonably should know' from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct contradiction with the intent and scope of the original definition, as the contradicted text includes a broader range of information that the recipient *should* have known was confidential, but the modified text only covers information the recipient *actually* knows is confidential. This inconsistency in the definition creates an in-text contradiction because what is deemed confidential under the original scope is no longer protected under the changed scope.

perturbation.original_text: 4. The Confidential Information, regardless of whether it is in oral or written, or partial or complete, form, shall be and remain the property of the Company, whether provided to or copied by Recipient. Recipient shall not make or have made any partial or complete copies or derivative works, whether in hand copy or electronic form, of any of the Confidential Information without the express prior authorization of the Company, and any legends or notices used by the Company in the Confidential Information shall be reproduced in full in all copies. Upon demand by the Company or termination of this Agreement, all materials bearing or disclosing any of the Confidential Information shall be delivered or returned to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. The Confidential Information, regardless of whether it is in oral or written, or partial or complete, form, shall be and remain the property of the Company, whether provided to or copied by Recipient. Recipient shall not make or have made any complete copies or derivative works, whether in hand copy or electronic form, of any of the Confidential Information without the express prior authorization of the Company, and any legends or notices used by the Company in the Confidential Information shall be reproduced in full in all copies. Upon demand by the Company or termination of this Agreement, all materials bearing or disclosing any of the Confidential Information shall be delivered or returned to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'partial copies' from the restrictions in Section 4, there is an in-text contradiction. The original paragraph stated all forms of copying are restricted, and with this omission, partial copies are now permitted, while the definition of Confidential Information in Section 1 still implies that all of it should be protected, regardless of whether it is a partial or complete copy. The agreement fails to clearly restrict the use of partial copies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information, regardless of whether it is in oral or written, or partial or complete, form, shall be and remain the property of the Company, whether provided to or copied by Recipient. Recipient shall not make or have made any complete copies or derivative works, whether in hand copy or electronic form, of any of the Confidential Information without the express prior authorization of the Company, and any legends or notices used by the Company in the Confidential Information shall be reproduced in full in all copies. Upon demand by the Company or termination of this Agreement, all materials bearing or disclosing any of the Confidential Information shall be delivered or returned to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 removes the phrase 'partial copies' from the restrictions, while the definition of Confidential Information (which is also shown as the contradicted text) explicitly states that it includes information in 'partial or complete' form. This creates a direct contradiction. The original text restricted both complete and partial copies, but the changed text only restricts complete copies, implying partial copies are now permitted. This conflicts with the broader definition of confidential information and its intended protection as the contradiction text mentions that all confidential information regardless of partial or complete form should remain the company's property. The contradiction is strong, because it directly changes the scope of protection and would lead to confusion about whether partial copies are allowed.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Recipient agrees not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party, except as specifically authorized by this Agreement or as specifically authorized by the Company in writing. Recipient agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence and agrees that it shall be used only by Recipient or its employees with a need- to- know for the contemplated purpose set forth above, and shall not be used for any other purpose or disclosed to any party who is not bound by a non-disclosure agreement with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Recipient agrees not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party, except as specifically authorized by this Agreement or as specifically authorized by the Company in writing. Recipient agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence and agrees that it shall be used only by Recipient or its employees, and shall not be used for any other purpose or disclosed to any party who is not bound by a non-disclosure agreement with the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'with a need-to-know for the contemplated purpose set forth above' from Section 2 creates an in-text contradiction. Now, Recipient's employees can access Confidential Information without needing it for the project, conflicting with the overall NDA's intent to restrict access. The section now ambiguously restricts disclosure to third-parties but not to employees, regardless of their need for the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party, except as specifically authorized by this Agreement or as specifically authorized by the Company in writing. Recipient agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence and agrees that it shall be used only by Recipient or its employees, and shall not be used for any other purpose or disclosed to any party who is not bound by a non-disclosure agreement with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2 restricts the use of Confidential Information to only employees 'with a need-to-know for the contemplated purpose'. The changed text removes this restriction, allowing any employee to access the information regardless of their need. This creates a contradiction because the changed text loosens the restriction on who can access confidential information, conflicting with the implicit intent of an NDA which is to restrict the access to confidential information to authorized individuals, creating an inconsistency within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 11. This Agreement sets forth the complete and exclusive agreement of the Parties regarding the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings and communications, oral or written, between the Parties regarding the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement is not, however, intended to limit any rights that the Company may have under trade secret, copyright, patent or other laws that may apply to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. This Agreement sets forth the complete and exclusive agreement of the Parties regarding the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings and communications, oral or written, between the Parties regarding the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement is also intended to limit any rights that the Company may have under trade secret, copyright, patent or other laws that may apply to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'not intended to limit' has been changed to 'is also intended to limit', creating an in-text contradiction in Section 11. This creates ambiguity by implying the agreement both protects the Company's rights under other laws and also limits those rights. The intent of the contract changes with this edit, and the company is now unsure whether to use other methods or stick to the document for dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement sets forth the complete and exclusive agreement of the Parties regarding the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings and communications, oral or written, between the Parties regarding the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement is also intended to limit any rights that the Company may have under trade secret, copyright, patent or other laws that may apply to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement was 'not intended to limit' the Company's rights under other laws. The changed text states the agreement 'is also intended to limit' those rights. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 11 because it now simultaneously suggests the agreement both protects and limits the company's rights, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about the enforceability of the agreement and other legal avenues available to the company. The change directly contradicts the previous intention and causes an inconsistency as defined in the instructions.
